mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut periods over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0%, labor market deterioration signals impending corporate earnings contraction, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 6.0% over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer demand expansion, which produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a Rank IC below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in less than 45% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the industrial production index during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side contraction that immediately penalizes equity valuations over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >50% of INDPRO decline months during tightening periods over a 4-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP establishes a baseline earnings trajectory that mechanically supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are negative in >55% of positive GDPC1 growth quarters over a 5-year walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above a 0.4% monthly threshold forces expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of months where CPIAUCSL MoM exceeds 0.4% over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining federal funds rate creates a soft landing environment that reliably produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to generate positive returns in >50% of occurrences over a 4-year walk-forward test, or interaction term Rank IC < 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in retail sales signal immediate consumer demand collapse, triggering rapid equity deleveraging and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines for two consecutive months
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >55% of double-dip retail sales months over a 3-year rolling window.
